arguments.description: Attaches postpositionally to a noun phrase or pronoun to define specificity and mark the phrase as the topicalized subject.

preceding_element.note: Accepts nominal hosts, including third-person plural pronouns like 'Amaute'.

following_element.note: Typically followed by a possessive construction or a nominal predicate phrase leading into a copula.

parallel_sentences.zol: Amaute pen ka innkuanpihte ahi uh hi.

pos_payload.usage_notes: Functions as a specifier that singles out the preceding nominal phrase as the focus or topic of the clause.
structural_constraints: Attaches strictly to the right edge of the targeted noun phrase projection.
In Universal Dependencies parsing, it projects a 'det' relation back to the head noun or pronoun ('Amaute') which serves as the core 'nsubj' of the copula predicate.
